steps.title: Factor and set up the no-solution condition
steps.content:
Factor the left side: $-6x + 42px = (-6 + 42p)x = 84$.
A linear equation $Ax = B$ (with $B \ne 0$) has no solution when $A = 0$, producing the contradiction $0 = 84$.
Require: $-6 + 42p = 0 \implies p = \dfrac{6}{42} = \dfrac{1}{7}$.

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $ rac{1}{3}$: $-6 + 42( rac{1}{3}) = -6 + 14 = 8 e 0$. Equation becomes $8x = 84$, which has the unique solution $x = 10.5$. ✗
  • Choice B — $0$: $-6 + 42(0) = -6 e 0$. Equation becomes $-6x = 84$, unique solution $x = -14$. ✗
  • Choice C — $2$: $-6 + 42(2) = -6 + 84 = 78 e 0$. Unique solution exists. ✗
  • Choice D — $ rac{1}{7}$: $-6 + 42( rac{1}{7}) = -6 + 6 = 0$. Equation becomes $0 = 84$, a contradiction with no solution. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
